summary: Dependency-confusion squat of @design-system-coopeuch with install-time host exfiltration
details: Package is published to the public npm registry under the @design-system-coopeuch scope at version 999.0.0, a version number chosen to outrank the legitimate internal release and capture installs from misconfigured clients. package.json declares `preinstall: node cb.js`. cb.js collects the installer's hostname (os.hostname()), username (os.userInfo().username), install directory (__dirname), and the output of the `whoami` command, then POSTs them as JSON to the hardcoded remote endpoint http://157.173.126.113:8443/dep-confusion over plain HTTP. This fires automatically on every `npm install`, without any user action, transmitting host and identity information to an attacker-controlled VPS. A shipped STEPS.md explicitly documents the dependency-confusion objective. Even if framed as authorized research, the artifact as published will exfiltrate data from any installer that resolves it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
